content.question: Whether a phased (felmenő) implementation of selection-age reform could be efficiently coordinated across the multiple regulatory instruments required (Nkt. amendment, ministerial decree, annual admission regulation, maintainer agreements) without creating transitory legal ambiguities or administrative burden that could deter reform.
content.reduction_path: would be reduced by a detailed implementation roadmap showing the statutory sequence, timing dependencies, and coordination mechanisms (e.g., lead roles, decision gates) required to manage a multi-year phase-in across layered regulatory sources.
